_meta.description: Authors identified as having 3 or fewer quotes in the DB, based on analysis of all_authors.json against the 13K quote corpus. These authors need a minimum of 10 quotes each to be useful in recommendations and packs.
_meta.how_to_verify: SELECT author, COUNT(*) as quote_count FROM quotes GROUP BY author HAVING COUNT(*) <= 3 ORDER BY quote_count ASC, author ASC;
_meta.action_required: Add 7-10 new verified quotes per author. Use prepare_quotes_dataset.py with targeted HuggingFace queries, or manually curate from primary sources.

authors_needing_quotes.name: Reggie Jackson (basketball, born 1990)
authors_needing_quotes.reason: Niche sports figure with only 1-2 attributable philosophical quotes. Name collision with baseball player creates DB confusion. Consider consolidating to 'Reggie Jackson (NBA)' or removing.

authors_needing_quotes.name: Mike Singletary (basketball)
authors_needing_quotes.reason: Ambiguous entry — label suggests a basketball figure distinct from the famous NFL coach Mike Singletary. Verified quotes for this specific person are not confirmed.

authors_needing_quotes.name: Joe Adcock
authors_needing_quotes.reason: 1950s baseball player. Only documented philosophical quote is a single line on perseverance. Insufficient corpus for a meaningful author profile.

authors_needing_quotes.name: Goi Nasu
authors_needing_quotes.reason: Contemporary Japanese writer known for a single widely-shared quote. Very little verified output beyond that one text.
authors_needing_quotes.note: If additional verified quotes cannot be found, keep as single-quote author and document as such.

authors_needing_quotes.name: Vernon Cooper
authors_needing_quotes.reason: Folk preacher with oral tradition only. Fewer than 3 quotations are verifiably attributed. No published works.

authors_needing_quotes.name: Tom Krause
authors_needing_quotes.reason: Motivational speaker with limited documented output. Most quotes are short maxims without a verifiable primary source.

authors_needing_quotes.name: Carrie Snow
authors_needing_quotes.reason: Stand-up comedian — very limited documented philosophical quotes. Most attributed lines are quips rather than reflective content.

authors_needing_quotes.name: Sam Ewing
authors_needing_quotes.reason: Humorist with a small number of verifiable attributions. Many quotes attributed to him online are misattributed.

authors_needing_quotes.name: John Acosta
authors_needing_quotes.reason: No verifiable publication record. Quotes attributed to him may be from multiple different people with the same name.
authors_needing_quotes.note: Investigate attribution. If unverifiable, remove from DB.

authors_needing_quotes.name: Marsha Petrie Sue
authors_needing_quotes.reason: Niche business speaker. Limited philosophical quote corpus relevant to MindScrolling's categories.

authors_needing_quotes.name: John Berry
authors_needing_quotes.reason: Jazz journalist. Quotes are niche and may not fit philosophy/mindfulness categories. Low supply of thematic quotes.

authors_needing_quotes.name: Volker Grassmuck
authors_needing_quotes.reason: Media scholar with limited popular quotes. Existing quotes are tech/policy-focused, not philosophical.

authors_needing_quotes.name: Libby Larsen
authors_needing_quotes.reason: Composer with very few documented philosophical statements. Narrow quote corpus.

authors_needing_quotes.name: Robert Griffin III
authors_needing_quotes.reason: NFL quarterback. Only 1-2 motivational quotes in circulation. Not aligned with philosophy/mindfulness theme.

authors_needing_quotes.name: Joe Adcock
authors_needing_quotes.reason: 1950s baseball player with a single documented quote.

authors_needing_quotes.name: Paavo Nurmi
authors_needing_quotes.reason: Finnish runner. Very few verified quotes. 'The mind is everything; the body is just muscles and bones' is widely shared but may be apocryphal.

authors_needing_quotes.name: Richard Garriott
authors_needing_quotes.reason: Video game designer. Very few philosophical quotes. Niche audience.

authors_needing_quotes.name: Fawn M. Brodie
authors_needing_quotes.reason: Biographer with scholarly quotes. Few short, impactful philosophical quotes.

authors_needing_quotes.name: Camillo Benso, Count of Cavour
authors_needing_quotes.reason: Italian statesman with a narrow quote corpus in English. Historical but not well-represented in HuggingFace datasets.

authors_needing_quotes.name: John Pierrakos
authors_needing_quotes.reason: Psychotherapist. Limited documented quotes; his work is primarily clinical rather than philosophical aphorism.

authors_needing_quotes.name: Léon Blum
authors_needing_quotes.reason: French politician. Political quotes available but few align with stoicism/philosophy/mindfulness categories.

authors_needing_quotes.name: Doris Mortman
authors_needing_quotes.reason: Novelist known for a single famous quote about inner beauty. Very narrow documented output.
authors_needing_quotes.note: Her one verified quote is high quality. Keep but do not expand.

authors_needing_quotes.name: Vidal Sassoon
authors_needing_quotes.reason: Hairdresser/businessman. Famous for one quote about work and the dictionary. Limited philosophical corpus.

authors_needing_quotes.name: Gordon Hinckley
authors_needing_quotes.reason: Religious leader with a moderate quote corpus, but most quotes are LDS-doctrinal rather than universally philosophical.

authors_needing_quotes.name: Bernadette Devlin
authors_needing_quotes.reason: Irish civil rights politician. Few quotes in HuggingFace corpora. Niche audience.

authors_needing_quotes.name: Cheng Yen
authors_needing_quotes.reason: Appears twice in authors table (duplicate). Quote corpus exists but is split across two author records. Consolidation required.
authors_needing_quotes.note: Merge all quotes from 'cheng-yen' and 'cheng-yen-2' into a single author record. Then add_quotes to reach 10.

authors_needing_quotes.name: Sam Keen
authors_needing_quotes.reason: Appears twice in authors table (duplicate). Same consolidation issue as Cheng Yen.

authors_needing_quotes.name: Etty Hillesum
authors_needing_quotes.reason: Appears twice in authors table (duplicate). Her diaries contain many quotable passages; quote corpus should be richer.
authors_needing_quotes.note: Rich primary source (diaries). After consolidation, add 10+ more quotes from 'An Interrupted Life' and 'Letters from Westerbork'.

authors_needing_quotes.name: Peter Abelard
authors_needing_quotes.reason: Medieval philosopher with a small popular quote corpus. Rich primary sources exist but are not well represented in HuggingFace datasets.

authors_needing_quotes.name: Walter Inglis Anderson
authors_needing_quotes.reason: American artist-hermit. His diaries contain beautiful passages but they are not widely indexed in digital quote corpora.

authors_needing_quotes.name: Moncure D. Conway
authors_needing_quotes.reason: Abolitionist minister. Historical figure with limited popular quote circulation.

authors_needing_quotes.name: William Ellery Channing
authors_needing_quotes.reason: Unitarian minister with moderate corpus. Strong on inner life and human dignity — aligns well with philosophy/reflection categories.

authors_needing_quotes.name: Hugh Miller
authors_needing_quotes.reason: Scottish geologist-writer. Very few popular quotes despite rich written output.

authors_needing_quotes.name: Alexander Chalmers
authors_needing_quotes.reason: Scottish biographer and editor. Essentially no philosophical quotes in modern circulation.

authors_needing_quotes.name: Doug Larson
authors_needing_quotes.reason: Newspaper columnist. Known for witty one-liners but no verified philosophical depth. Most quotes attributed to him online are misattributed.
